question: Explain the inventory of coolant in the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) and the management of its chemistry.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and comply with relevant U.S. regulatory requirements to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance. These materials are subject to rigorous testing and quality assurance measures, with detailed specifications provided in referenced tables and sections.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is determined, and leakage rates are monitored through RCS inventory balance and comparison with the containment evacuation system (CES) flow. The leakage detection systems are designed to be reliable, redundant, and sensitive to support safety analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their compliance with ASME BPVC standards, and highlights corrosion protection measures such as weld cladding and material selection for compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains water chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

edges.description: Section A describes the materials and construction standards ensuring the integrity of the Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary, which is directly related to Section B's discussion of detecting leakage from this boundary as part of managing the coolant inventory and chemistry.

Section B references Section A by indicating that the material of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are part of the pressurizer, is described in Section 5.2 (Section A), thus connecting the detailed component description to the general material requirements.

Section A specifies the materials and regulatory compliance for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which structurally supports and contains the coolant inventory described in Section B's summary of the Reactor Coolant System.

Section B provides detailed descriptions of pressurizer materials and components that are referenced in Section A's discussion of the Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary, connecting the physical design to leakage detection considerations.

Section B provides a general overview of the RCS design and components, while Section A specifies details about leakage detection within that system, particularly noting the absence of traditional components that would affect coolant inventory and chemistry management.

Section A provides detailed specifications of the pressurizer materials and components, which are part of the broader RCS described in Section B, thereby elaborating on specific elements mentioned in the general system overview.
